lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Five forty-three in the morning. Burnt coffee on the machine, still cycling. The kind of fatigue that settles into hospital furniture after years of absorbing bad news. Theo stands at his locker. The door is open. Nothing taken out.

lines.speaker: SISTER AGNES
lines.text: Ward four just called. Mr. Callahan's oxygen sat dropped to eighty-one. They've moved him to observation, but Fenwick says if it continues they won't have capacity to manage him there.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Ward four. The cut ward. The one that lost its second resus bay six weeks ago when Harte signed the consolidation order.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: What time did the drop start?

lines.speaker: SISTER AGNES
lines.text: Twenty minutes ago. He'd been stable at ninety-four since midnight.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Stable. The way a building is stable while something load-bearing is being quietly removed.

lines.speaker: SISTER AGNES
lines.text: To the night administrator. Not to Harte. She said she didn't want to make the call without more numbers.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text:
More numbers. Three months ago Theo would have understood that. Now he understands something else: the numbers are already there. Months of collecting — four pages, single-spaced, incident reports cross-referenced with the diversion logs. A patient name on each page. Mr. Callahan's name appearing twice in the last three weeks.

The form is in his locker.

He can see it from where he's standing.

lines.speaker: SISTER AGNES
lines.text: Knowing and filing are different things.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She says it without judgment, which makes it worse. Not pressing. Just naming the distance between where he is and where the form requires him to be.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: If I file now and he stabilizes, they'll say the policy never caused harm. They'll use his recovery against the complaint.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: No answer. His eyes settle on the open locker, on the folded pages just visible under his coat. The name not yet placed after his own. The date still blank.

lines.speaker: SISTER AGNES
lines.text: I'll be in the chapel until six. If you need a witness for the timestamp.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text:
She goes. The door behind her stays ajar. The machine continues its cycle. Theo stays where he is.

He checked the pen before this shift started — not from nerves, just a habit carried over from anything that might matter. Pen in his coat pocket. Form in his locker. Mr. Callahan on ward four, an oxygen saturation of eighty-one, a body trying to hold on past the point where the building can support it.

Theo pulls his coat from the hook. Shuts the locker door.

Goes back to the floor.
